title: Guarantee generated target parity and freshness

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement #1358 by making generated-target freshness proof cover both Python and TypeScript generated outputs when relevant source or generated surfaces change.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not directly edit generated targets as a substitute for generator output. Do not claim generated-CLI single-source satisfaction from Python-only proof. Keep the check cheap: use freshness/hash/check evidence rather than regenerating on every AW call.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: How to expose parity/freshness evidence inside existing generator/check/proof-selection surfaces.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires new generic command-generation APIs, cross-repo changes, or broad generated target layout changes.
canonical_core.next_action: Inspect generator/check freshness surfaces and add cheap Python/TypeScript parity proof.
proof_expectations: uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py implement --changed <changed paths> --task "Implement #1358 generated target parity and freshness" --format json
completion_criteria: Generated-package proof or proof selection exposes both Python and TypeScript generated target freshness obligations for relevant changes.
Freshness proof remains cheap and does not require regenerating on every AW call.
Stale generated outputs remain detectable by generator --check/static proof.
Parent #1354 remains open unless command-generation support and final dogfooding closeout are also satisfied.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Guarantee generated target parity and freshness.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: No generated-target hand edits as substitute for generator output; no Python-only generated freshness claims.
intent.latitude: Choose compact parity evidence and focused tests inside existing AW proof/check surfaces.
intent.escalation: Escalate if generic support must move to command-generation or if target generation architecture changes are needed.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #1354 generated-CLI single-source boundary across Python and TypeScript generated targets.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After this lane closes, shape/complete command-generation support and final dogfooding closeout.

iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: yes; planning closeout recorded explicit proof input.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for this archived slice; reopen only if new evidence invalidates the proof.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Guarantee generated target parity and freshness
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Relevant generated-CLI changes should not leave Python and TypeScript generated outputs out of sync or unproven.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Expose parity/freshness obligations for both generated target families through existing proof/check surfaces.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check whether the proof output can make a Python-only freshness claim, and whether TypeScript staleness remains cheaply detectable.

execution_bounds.max changed files: About 7 files; ask before touching generated outputs or external command-generation code.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused tests, check_generated_command_packages.py, generate_command_packages.py --check, AW changed-path implement proof selection.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing generated target layout, adding cross-repo command-generation APIs, or modifying generated outputs directly.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated target files, command-generation package internals, unrelated runtime primitives, or parent closeout claims.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, #1358, #1354 decomposition, generator/check scripts, AW proof selection, and focused tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue #1358: add cheap Python/TypeScript generated target parity/freshness proof without editing generated targets directly.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No Python-only proof claims; no direct generated-target edits as substitute for generation.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Compact proof packet shape, tests, and integration into existing check/proof surfaces.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Generic support must move to command-generation or target architecture changes are needed.
